level_signals: 8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, enterprise sales, or growth

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, enterprise sales, or growth, ideally in a technology or SaaS environment
Native or business-level Japanese fluency and deep cultural familiarity with Japan's business environment
Proven track record sourcing, structuring, and closing enterprise or government partnerships in Japan
Experience working with or selling to Japanese public sector institutions, large enterprises, or through channel/reseller networks
Strong communicator who can adapt messaging for technical developers, non-technical operators, C-suite executives, and government stakeholders alike
Self-starter with a bias for action; comfortable with ambiguity and excited to build market presence from the ground up
Data-driven mindset — you measure what matters and use insights to iterate quickly
Genuine passion for the future of software creation and democratizing access to building software

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in developer tools, coding education, SaaS platforms, or digital transformation consulting
Existing enterprise, government, or channel partner relationships in Japan you'd bring to the role
Experience managing or building partner programs at scale, including reseller programs with tiered incentives
Familiarity with Japanese government digital initiatives such as DX (Digital Transformation) mandates or e-Gov platforms
Business-level English fluency for collaboration with Replit's global team

scope.surface: Replit's commercial presence in Japan — enterprise, government, education, channel/reseller, and community/PLG
scope.business_model: B2B and B2G (enterprise and government) + B2B channel/reseller + B2B2C education; SaaS platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Deal pipeline volume and close rate
Market presence and brand recognition in Japan
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end partnership development with enterprise accounts, government agencies, and public sector institutions in Japan
Identify, recruit, and activate resellers, system integrators, and channel partners who can distribute Replit's products in Japan
Build partnerships with Japanese universities, coding bootcamps, and educational institutions
Drive bottom-up adoption among Japanese builders through community engagement, developer relations, and PLG initiatives
Represent Replit at major Japanese technology conferences, trade shows, and industry events
Build and manage a robust deal pipeline, tracking opportunities from prospecting through close
Report on partnership and growth metrics

green_flags: Clear ownership scope — 'own' end-to-end across enterprise, government, education, and PLG
High-impact, externally-facing role with direct market-building mandate
Generous benefits package (health, dental, vision, parental leave, FTO, wellness stipend)
Reports directly to International Partnerships Lead — clear line of authority
Encourages candidates from underrepresented and non-traditional backgrounds
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed — 'Competitive Salary & Equity' is vague
401(k) noted as 'US Only' — unclear what Japan-equivalent retirement benefits look like
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization support for Japan
Role is fully onsite in Tokyo with no remote option stated
Benefits section appears US-centric (401k, commuter benefits labelled 'In-Office Only') — applicability to Tokyo-based employee is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and 'Lead' title)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and partnership targets)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type (stated as 'full-time role based in Tokyo, Japan'; interpreted as onsite)
ambiguities: Benefits section includes US-specific items (401k with 4% match 'US Only', commuter benefits 'In-Office Only') — unclear which apply to a Tokyo-based hire; may indicate benefits are partially or fully US-centric
Work authorization and visa sponsorship for Japan not mentioned anywhere in the JD
The archetype is closest to 'BizOps / Strategy & Ops' or 'Growth (non-PM)'; this role is primarily a market/partnership development lead, not a classic ops or product role — confidence is medium
